title: Merge Intervals
description:
Given an array of `intervals` where `intervals[i] = [start_i, end_i]`, merge all overlapping intervals, and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals that cover all the intervals in the input.

Two intervals overlap if one starts before or when the other ends.

**Constraints:**
- 1 <= intervals.length <= 10^4
- intervals[i].length == 2
- 0 <= start_i <= end_i <= 10^4

examples.explanation: Intervals [1,3] and [2,6] overlap, so they merge into [1,6].
Intervals [1,4] and [4,5] are considered overlapping (they share endpoint 4).

editorial: Sort intervals by start time. Then iterate through them, maintaining a `merged` result list. For each interval, if it overlaps with the last interval in `merged` (i.e., its start <= last interval's end), extend the last interval's end to the max of both ends. Otherwise, append it as a new interval. Sorting takes O(n log n); the merge pass is O(n). Total: O(n log n) time, O(n) space.

rexHints.level1: Sorting the intervals first makes it much easier to detect overlaps.
rexHints.level2: After sorting by start time, two adjacent intervals overlap if the second one's start is <= the first one's end.
rexHints.level3: Sort by start. Initialize result = [intervals[0]]. For each subsequent interval [s,e]: if s <= result.last[1], set result.last[1] = max(result.last[1], e). Else push [s,e] to result. Return result.
